title: Agent Workbench
description: A desktop control plane for coordinating Claude, Codex, Gemini, shell, and Python sessions from one operator surface. PTY terminals, voice input, persistent session state, and an operator panel make multi-agent work visible enough to steer.
challenge: Running several AI coding agents at once means juggling separate terminals with no shared view of what each one is doing. Agent Workbench turns that sprawl into one controllable workspace: spawn the agents, watch their output, and direct the next move without losing the thread.
approach: Built on Electron and Next.js with a Node PTY sidecar, so every agent is a real terminal child process with full I/O, not a fragile API wrapper. The operator panel handles spawn, kill, and directed tool calls; push-to-talk voice routes instructions from anywhere in the app; local state keeps the system private and inspectable.

metrics.label: Concurrent agent types (Claude, Codex, Gemini, shell, Python)
State and logs persist at ~/.agent-workbench; no cloud
Push-to-talk operator panel; hold Space anywhere to dictate

outcomes: A shared control plane for multiple AI agents and terminal sessions.
Voice-to-agent dispatch from any context in the app.
Persistent sessions that keep exited agents visible for output review.

focusNarratives.product-program-leadership: Agent Workbench turns multi-agent development from terminal sprawl into a product surface. Persistent sessions, clear agent states, voice input, reviewable output, and an operator panel give the workflow enough structure that a human can steer it without slowing it down.
focusNarratives.ai-automation: Agent Workbench brings several AI runtimes into one local-first workspace. Instructions route to the right agent, session context stays visible, PTY controls keep execution inspectable, and the operator remains in the loop when judgment matters.

title: Polymarket Trading Bot
description: A prediction-market signal pipeline for Polymarket sports markets. It ingests markets, normalizes pricing inputs, scores edge and liquidity, and keeps execution gated until the model earns trust through review.
challenge: Prediction-market trading demands more than a clever signal. Sportsbook odds, CLOB depth, timing, liquidity, and risk state all have to resolve into an auditable decision before any execution step deserves authority.
approach: Structured the pipeline around ingestion, normalization, model scoring, and disabled execution. The decision engine emits sized order intents with signal attribution, while the live-trading path stays off until paper review proves the model is worth trusting.

metrics.label: Unit tests passing across contract, ingestion, model, and risk
External dependencies in core model engine (pure stdlib)
Phase 3 execution disabled by default; validate first

outcomes: A full path from market discovery to sized order intent.
Auditable decision packets with signal attribution.
A validation-first design that keeps live execution disabled by default.

focusNarratives.ai-automation: The Polymarket bot handles the pieces that should be systematic: market ingestion, normalization, edge scoring, liquidity checks, timing signals, and sizing. Execution stays gated, because the point is not to automate risk faster; it is to make the signal auditable before it gets authority.

title: Navore Market
description: A USDA-backed San Diego marketplace connecting local producers and consumers through neighborhood hubs, mobile ordering, and practical local-food operations.
challenge: Local food demand is real, but the system around it is fragmented: producer discovery, ordering, trust, fulfillment, and repeat demand usually live in separate places. Navore has to make those pieces behave like one marketplace.
approach: I help shape strategic partnerships, the platform roadmap, AI-enabled initiatives, producer relationships, hub operations, grant execution, logistics, analytics, and customer feedback loops. The marketplace and operating model are built together, because one without the other does not hold.

metrics.label: USDA-backed funding
Initial operating market
Marketplace and hub operations

outcomes: Launched a real local-food marketplace and La Jolla neighborhood hub.
USDA-backed funding supports expansion of the model.
Built operating workflows that connect demand, producers, fulfillment, and growth.

focusNarratives.strategy: Navore Market is a USDA-backed local-food marketplace in San Diego. I helped shape the market model around neighborhood hubs, producer relationships, grant milestones, customer segments, strategic partnerships, platform roadmap, and growth loops that can turn local-food demand into something more durable than a one-off storefront.
focusNarratives.operations: Navore has to hold up on the ground. Producer workflows, hub logistics, fulfillment routines, customer support, grant execution, analytics, and performance metrics all have to connect cleanly enough for the model to repeat beyond one neighborhood.
focusNarratives.product-program-leadership: Navore is both a marketplace product and an operating system for local food. Customer experience, producer onboarding, mobile ordering, fulfillment, roadmap tradeoffs, and feedback loops have to evolve together so the marketplace improves as a live service, not as disconnected features.
focusNarratives.marketplace-venture-building: Navore is the venture-building case in its most direct form: supply, demand, trust, logistics, funding, and neighborhood adoption all have to move together. My role translates that 0 to 1 complexity into a clearer marketplace model, producer relationships, hub operations, and a scalable operating playbook.

title: Metis
description: A personal AI operating system that runs agents as a governed, multi-session workforce across two machines. Specialized lanes, persistent memory, task governance, and a control-center UI keep the system compounding instead of starting cold every session.
challenge: Useful agents need more than a chat window. They need memory that compounds, task coordination, live system visibility, safety gates, and a way to operate across machines without stale sessions corrupting shared work.
approach: I built a local-first operating layer with specialized agent lanes (generation, research, review, governance), persistent memory, a governed task lifecycle with leases and fencing tokens, and a Next.js control center for real-time visibility. The portable core is published as the Metis Framework; the private operating repo layers identity, projects, and integrations on top.

metrics.label: Specialized local-model lanes (generation, research, review, governance), steered by one orchestrating model
Machines coordinated; an always-on host plus an on-demand peer sharing one model host
Governed tasks completed through the forward-only state machine
Private MLX inference on-device; memory and state never leave the machines
Source-available framework published for others to vendor and run

outcomes: A governed agent workforce that compounds across sessions instead of starting fresh.
Real-time control center with task boards, agent sessions, and typed decision queues.
Published the portable core as an open-source framework others can vendor into their own repos.

focusNarratives.operations: Metis is my private operating layer for running projects with AI agents across two machines. Task governance, leases, health checks, lane routing, and escalation rules keep the automation reliable enough to run unattended overnight.
focusNarratives.product-program-leadership: Metis behaves like a product even though it is personally operated infrastructure. I scope releases, design the control-center UI, sequence agent capabilities, define done gates, and publish the portable core as an open-source framework.
focusNarratives.ai-automation: Metis combines local-model lanes, persistent memory, governed task lifecycles, and multi-machine orchestration into one operating system. The framework is source-available; the private repo layers identity, projects, and integrations on top.

title: HealthRise EHR & Revenue Operations
description: A healthtech environment spanning EHR software, revenue cycle management, and consulting delivery. I helped connect ML/AI integration, strategic planning, financial forecasting, product execution, and global delivery routines.
challenge: Healthcare technology fails when product ambition ignores clinical workflow, reimbursement reality, and delivery capacity. HealthRise needed EHR and revenue-operations initiatives that could move from roadmap to shipped workflow without losing the operators who had to use them.
approach: As Technical Program Manager, I drove ML/AI integration across global healthcare teams while supporting strategic planning and financial forecasting. The work cut across requirements translation, Scrum delivery, UX/UI testing, roadmap coordination, release planning, stakeholder feedback, and global delivery execution.

metrics.label: Clinical workflow technology
Revenue cycle management
Operational advisory services

outcomes: Drove ML/AI integration across global delivery teams.
Supported strategic planning and financial forecasting across the program portfolio.
Connected clinical workflows, revenue cycle operations, and product delivery.

focusNarratives.strategy: HealthRise sat at the intersection of EHR software, revenue cycle operations, consulting delivery, and healthcare workflow improvement. I connected product direction, strategic planning, financial forecasting, delivery priorities, and stakeholder needs into initiatives that made sense commercially and could survive implementation.
focusNarratives.operations: Healthcare-product ambition only matters if it can survive real implementation. I translated clinical workflows, RCM handoffs, team capacity, global delivery routines, and rollout constraints into plans teams could execute and customers could adopt.
focusNarratives.product-program-leadership: HealthRise was a SaaS and services environment where product decisions had direct operational consequences. I worked across the product development lifecycle: requirements translation, Scrum delivery, UX/UI testing, roadmap coordination, release planning, stakeholder feedback, and global delivery execution to move EHR and revenue-operations initiatives from idea to shipped workflow.
focusNarratives.ai-automation: At HealthRise, AI had to fit inside healthcare operations rather than sit beside them. I helped drive ML/AI integration across global delivery teams, focusing on model-assisted workflows that could support EHR and revenue-cycle processes without ignoring usability, implementation risk, or delivery constraints.

title: Johnson Matthey Enterprise Transformation
description: Enterprise transformation inside a global specialty chemicals company. I led strategic transformation initiatives across North America, drove measurable operating improvement, and built the North America Government Affairs function from zero.
challenge: Johnson Matthey needed transformation programs that could move a complex global business, not just describe a future state. The work required stakeholder alignment, scoped initiatives, process discipline, and new capabilities that could survive after the program ended.
approach: I translated ambiguous corporate priorities into transformation programs, execution rhythms, measurable efficiency targets, and cross-functional cadences. The portfolio included 12 strategic transformation initiatives, process-efficiency improvement, and the zero-to-one buildout of North America Government Affairs.

metrics.label: Enterprise transformation programs led
Process efficiency improvement
North America Government Affairs function

outcomes: Led 12 enterprise transformation programs across North America.
Drove measurable process-efficiency improvement.
Founded and headed the North America Government Affairs function from scratch.

focusNarratives.strategy: Johnson Matthey needed transformation programs that could move a global specialty chemicals business, not just describe a future state. I translated ambiguous corporate priorities into scoped initiatives, stakeholder alignment, measurable efficiency targets, and the case for building a North America Government Affairs function from zero.
focusNarratives.operations: The enterprise changes had to become durable routines inside a complex organization. I led execution across 12 programs, drove process-efficiency improvement, coordinated cross-functional cadences, and helped new capabilities become part of how the business actually worked.
focusNarratives.product-program-leadership: Johnson Matthey required program leadership in a complex enterprise environment. I translated ambiguous priorities into scoped initiatives, built execution rhythms, coordinated cross-functional stakeholders, tracked outcomes, and kept transformation tied to measurable operating improvement rather than slideware.

title: Plotted
description: An editorial data-visualization gallery where each piece is a self-standing visual story built on publicly available datasets. D3 interactivity meets authored visual language.
challenge: Most data dashboards optimize for density and utility. Plotted asks a different question: can a visualization be a story worth reading on its own, with real interactivity, honest sourcing, and a visual language that earns attention?
approach: Each piece starts from a publicly available dataset (OWID, NASA, BLS) and gets a bespoke D3 component with its own layout, palette, and narrative arc. The gallery runs on Astro as a static site with a warm editorial design system.

metrics.label: Completed pieces, gallery deploy in flight (life expectancy, exoplanets)
Interactive SVG on a static site; no server needed
Every piece cites its source and license

outcomes: A visual language that treats data pieces as authored stories, not dashboards.
Interactive D3 visualizations running as pure static HTML.
A gallery format designed to grow one piece at a time.

focusNarratives.product-program-leadership: Plotted is a gallery product with a clear editorial voice. Each piece gets its own layout and narrative treatment, built on a reusable Astro + D3 system that keeps production cost low enough to publish steadily.
focusNarratives.ai-automation: The gallery is part of my broader toolkit: AI-assisted research and data prep feed into hand-crafted D3 visualizations. The system handles the ingestion; the editorial judgment stays human.

title: Personal Portfolio
description: A living portfolio for projects, writing, and public proof-of-work. Structured project data powers multiple pages so the site can evolve with the work instead of aging into a static resume.

focusNarratives.product-program-leadership: This site is a living portfolio rather than a static resume. Reusable content models, filtered work surfaces, project detail pages, public proof-of-work, and live review loops make it a small product system, not a one-time redesign.
focusNarratives.ai-automation: The portfolio is part of my AI-assisted operating loop. Browser comments turn into implementation tasks, structured project data powers multiple views, and agent-supported workflows keep the site current without turning every update into manual website maintenance.

title: Globalization Photojournalism Exhibit
description: A photojournalism exhibit exploring globalization through fieldwork in Tibet and Mongolia.
challenge: Globalization is often discussed through abstract economic and political language. The project sought to document how it appeared in lived environments and everyday experience.
approach: I conducted fieldwork in Tibet and Mongolia, using photography and firsthand observation to build a visual narrative around globalization and cultural change.

metrics.label: Fieldwork regions
Final public format
Research and storytelling method

outcomes: Produced a photojournalism exhibit based on international fieldwork.
Translated an abstract global issue into a visual narrative.
Documented perspectives and environments across Tibet and Mongolia.
